page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn8ia.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For acceleration, the instance includes a single NVIDIA L20 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which provides 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the dedicated AMD EPYC processor cores and the high-capacity NVIDIA L20 GPU, this instance offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring significant graphics memory and parallel processing power. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for tasks that benefit from a large VRAM footprint without requiring massive local storage. Appropriate workloads include GPU-accelerated database operations, machine learning inference, data science tasks, and specialized rendering workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn8ia.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T34 CPU cores running at 3.4 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory and a single NVIDIA L20 GPU from the Ada Lovelace family, which delivers 48 GB of VRAM. The instance does not feature local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance is designed for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources and substantial GPU memory. It is well-suited for machine learning inference, data science pipelines, and parallel computing tasks where high VRAM capacity is critical.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and an NVIDIA L20 GPU with forty-eight gigabytes of video memory.
